verdict: Well-structured skill with correct intelligence placement and lean customization surface. One runtime bug ({output_file} undefined) must be fixed before shipping. Update mode is undocumented, and a dual-output distillate would transform this from a one-shot document generator into a vocabulary service for the entire pipeline.

findings.title: {output_file} undefined in Stage 3 script invocation — runtime failure
findings.description: {output_file} is not declared in customize.toml or any BMad config. At runtime the LLM will leave it unexpanded or invent a path. Also output_dir is declared in customize.toml but never referenced by SKILL.md, so team overrides silently no-op.
findings.fix: Replace {output_file} with a resolvable expression using {workflow.output_dir}. Consider adding output_filename scalar or renaming output_dir to glossary_output_path per BMad *_output_path convention.

findings.title: Dual-output distillate missing — glossary only produces human document
findings.description: The glossary is the canonical term reference for all downstream documents. Without a machine-readable glossary-distillate.json, every subsequent skill re-parses the full markdown or ignores the glossary entirely.
findings.fix: In Stage 4, after saving D-03, write glossary-distillate.json ({term: definition} map) to the same output directory. Add distillate_path to headless return schema.

findings.title: Intent gate fires after scan script runs — wasted work for wrong-skill users
findings.description: Stage 1a runs the scan script before 1b confirms intent. A confused user waits for file-system scanning before being redirected. Violates intent-before-ingestion pattern.
findings.fix: Swap 1a and 1b: confirm intent first, then run scan. In headless, skip the intent gate.

findings.title: Update mode undocumented end-to-end
findings.description: update is listed as a valid arg but has no dedicated stage description. How new terms merge with existing ones, whether duplicates are surfaced, and whether revision history is auto-updated are all unanswered.
findings.fix: Add 3-4 sentence Update Mode subsection covering: load existing D-03 as baseline, present diff of new vs existing terms, merge non-duplicates, append revision history row. Add update path to headless contract.

findings.title: Intelligence leak: term classification via regex in scan script
findings.description: Regex patterns decide what content MEANS (whether a string is a domain term). This is semantic classification that belongs in the LLM, not a script. Produces false positives/negatives the LLM silently corrects.
findings.fix: Limit scan script to structural extraction (return all candidate strings matching surface patterns). Move is-this-a-meaningful-term? judgment to Stage 2 Discovery prompt.

findings.title: workflow_type metadata mismatch — simple-utility vs 4-stage workflow
findings.description: Prepass classifier tagged as simple-utility. Actual skill has 4 stages, decision log, resume/update/validate modes, parallel-lens menu, compaction flushes, headless contract. Misclassification confuses downstream tooling.
findings.fix: Ensure workflow_type reflects multi-stage-workflow classification. May need prepass heuristic adjustment.

findings.title: Scanner script hardcoded — not in customize.toml
findings.description: validation_script is customizable but scan-glossary-sources.py is hardcoded. Teams with custom term extraction pipelines must fork the skill.
findings.fix: Add scan_script scalar to customize.toml. Reference as {workflow.scan_script} in Stage 1.

findings.title: Parallel-lens menu labels opaque for first-time users
findings.description: [A] Advanced / [P] Party Mode / [C] Continue — no inline descriptions. First-time users don't know what Party Mode means or what Advanced challenges.
findings.fix: Add one-line descriptions: [A] Advanced — challenge definitions, find missing terms / [P] Party Mode — multi-reviewer perspective / [C] Continue — proceed to save.

findings.title: Compaction flush saves count only — actual terms lost on compaction
findings.description: Flush writes term count and category summary but not actual terms. If compaction fires mid-discovery, the full term list is lost and must be re-elicited.
findings.fix: Change flush to write discovered terms (term + definition + category) to decision log, not just count.

findings.title: Candidate extraction fails silently for non-Japanese/English sources
findings.description: Only Japanese quote patterns and English capitalized phrases are extracted. Vietnamese or other language sources produce 0 candidates silently.
findings.fix: Add language-neutral fallback (e.g., **bold** or _italic_ markdown markers). Add Stage 2 note when candidate_count==0 and source_count>0.

findings.title: Empty abbreviation table not handled
findings.description: Validator checks 略語一覧 section exists but not whether it's populated. A project with no abbreviations produces a confusing empty table.
findings.fix: Add Stage 3 LLM judgment: if no abbreviations collected, omit section or add placeholder note. Validator REQUIRED_SECTIONS check for 略語一覧 should be conditional.

findings.title: Missing tests for scan-glossary-sources.py
findings.description: validate-glossary.py has 8 tests. scan-glossary-sources.py has zero. State routing (fresh/resume/update) and frontmatter parsing are non-trivial logic paths.
findings.fix: Add test-scan-glossary-sources.py covering: fresh project, resume state, update state, candidate extraction.

findings.title: Prescriptive Discovery framing — outcome statement preferred
findings.description: For each term, capture: Term/Definition/Category is a schema that earns its place, but framing is mildly prescriptive.
findings.fix: Trim to outcome: Capture term, project-specific definition, and optional category for each.

findings.title: Headless-contract carve-out may be premature at 40 lines
findings.description: SKILL.md is 96 lines, under the 130-line ceiling. Headless contract (40 lines) could be inlined. But the carve-out works standalone and is correctly referenced.
findings.fix: Optional: inline as ## Headless Contract section. No strong pressure.

findings.title: No on_complete hook for downstream orchestration
findings.description: Headless contract returns paths for chaining but no hook scalar exists for post-completion automation.
findings.fix: Add on_complete = '' scalar to customize.toml. Low priority — return schema covers most cases.

findings.title: Party Mode label may not localize well
findings.description: Party Mode is English slang that translates poorly to Japanese/Vietnamese communication language.
findings.fix: Use language-neutral label like [P] Multi-lens review.

findings.title: Revision history date not auto-populated in Stage 4
findings.description: Stage 4 mentions updating frontmatter but not populating 改訂履歴 date column.
findings.fix: Add to Stage 4: Populate 改訂履歴 date with today's date.

findings.title: No adjacent-skill suggestions at intent gate
findings.description: If wrong skill, suggest the right one is outcome-based but common confusions (API glossary → requirements) could use a concrete example.
findings.fix: Add parenthetical: (e.g., if user wants API reference, suggest hbc-create-requirements [REQ]).

strengths: Inline workflow: all 4 stages in 96-line SKILL.md — clean, under ceiling
Intelligence placement mostly correct: structural checks in scripts, judgment in LLM
Headless contract precise and well-specified with blocked reasons
Decision-log workspace pattern correctly applied at compaction-flush points
Customization surface lean and correctly shaped — no booleans, no identity fields
Pre-pass/post-pass script bracket keeps LLM context lean
Soft-gate elicitation well placed in Stage 2
Test coverage for validator is thorough (8 cases)
